channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli strikes kill nine in Lebanon as Hezbollah fires rockets over border
item.description: At least nine people were killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, including two paramedics whose ambulance was hit, as Hezbollah fired rockets into northern Israel. The violence tests a partial ceasefire agreed on Monday that aimed to halt attacks on Beirut in exchange for Hezbollah refraining from cross-border strikes.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump appoints Bill Pulte as acting US intelligence chief
item.description: President Donald Trump has appointed businessman and federal housing regulator Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence, replacing Tulsi Gabbard. Pulte, who has no intelligence experience, faces skepticism from both Republican and Democratic lawmakers over his qualifications for overseeing 18 intelligence agencies including the CIA and NSA.

item.title: 🇺🇸 FBI fatally shoots hostage-taker after 12-hour California standoff
item.description: FBI agents shot and killed a man who had been holding 10 hostages inside a building in Bakersfield, California, ending a 12-hour standoff. All hostages were rescued unharmed. The incident began with a bomb threat at an office housing a bank branch and school district office.

item.title: 🇹🇷🇮🇩 Turkey and Indonesia discuss $10bn trade goal amid defence cooperation
item.description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan met Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto in Jakarta to discuss expanding cooperation in defence, energy, transportation and halal food sectors. The two countries are working toward a bilateral trade volume target of $10 billion, following an agreement reached in April 2025.

item.title: 💰 US announces new tariffs over forced labour concerns
item.description: The US announced new tariffs affecting multiple trading partners over forced labour concerns. The move comes after the Supreme Court struck down many of President Trump's previous duties in February.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇰🇼 Iran Strikes Kuwait Airport, Killing One as Ceasefire Tested
item.description: Iran launched drone strikes on Kuwait's main airport, killing one person and injuring dozens. Tehran said the attack was retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island, as Trump stated ceasefire talks are ongoing.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Ukrainian Drones Hit St Petersburg Oil Terminal Ahead of Economic Forum
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck oil storage facilities near St Petersburg as Putin's flagship economic forum was set to open. The attack comes days before the Russian president is scheduled to address the event.

item.title: 🇬🇧✈️ Three British Navy Personnel Killed in Helicopter Crash During Training
item.description: An investigation is underway after three British navy personnel died in a helicopter crash during a training exercise near Sourton in Devon, England.

item.title: 🦠🇨🇩 DRC Ebola Outbreak May Have Started as Early as January, WHO Says
item.description: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said the Ebola virus 'had a big head start' in the Democratic Republic of Congo, with the outbreak potentially beginning months ago. The virus is spreading across borders.

item.title: 🇲🇿🇿🇦 Mozambique Says Five Citizens Killed in Xenophobic Attacks in South Africa
item.description: Mozambique reported that five of its citizens were killed in xenophobic attacks in South Africa, highlighting ongoing regional tensions over migration and violence.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇧🇷 China lifts Brazilian beef ban but imposes 55% surtax quota
item.description: China removed foot-and-mouth disease restrictions on Brazilian beef imports but applied a 55% surtax through quota limits. The move comes as trade relations between Beijing and Brasilia evolve.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 US urges Europe and Canada to strengthen NATO air and naval forces
item.description: NATO military headquarters called on member nations to assign more capabilities to alliance operations. The request comes amid ongoing concerns about Russian aggression and European defense readiness.

item.title: 🛡️ NATO counter-drone tests in Latvia reveal gaps in European defenses
item.description: Drone technology remains ahead of countermeasures at a NATO range in Latvia, with interceptors needing perfect performance to prevent losses. The tests highlight ongoing challenges in European air defense.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇪🇺 Ukraine seeks EU fund to fill Patriot missile gap
item.description: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha called on Europe to use the newly unblocked European Peace Facility to purchase US Patriot systems and missiles. Zelensky threatened to fire officials over delays in missile supplies.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iran-Backed Iraqi Militias Begin Disarming Amid US Pressure
item.description: Powerful Iran-backed militias in Iraq say they will start handing in weapons to authorities, a significant shift as the US-Iran conflict tests its ceasefire. The move signals potential de-escalation in the region.

item.title: 🚀 Blue Origin Rocket Explodes During Launch, Key Components Survive
item.description: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin confirmed its New Glenn rocket exploded during launch, but fuel tanks and critical launch pad parts were spared. The failure marks a setback for the company's orbital ambitions.

item.title: 🔴 NASA Declares Mars Maven Spacecraft Dead After Six Months of Silence
item.description: NASA has officially declared its Mars Maven spacecraft dead after losing contact six months ago. The orbiter had been studying the Martian atmosphere since 2014.

item.title: 🛢️ Shell Continued Oil Extraction Despite Nigeria Pipeline Pollution Evidence
item.description: Documents reveal Shell pumped oil through a Nigerian pipeline for years despite evidence of pollution. The company says the documents ignore context of the complex operating environment.

item.title: 🔥 Delhi Hotel Fire Kills at Least 21, Many Foreign Nationals Among Victims
item.description: A fire at a hotel in New Delhi killed at least 21 people, including many foreign nationals who had travelled to India for medical treatment or to accompany relatives.

item.title: 🇳🇴 Norway joins French nuclear deterrence pact as trust in US security guarantees wavers
item.description: Norway became the ninth country to sign onto France's nuclear deterrence framework, marking a significant shift in European security thinking as doubts grow about American commitment to NATO.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky threatens to fire officials over delays in Patriot missile contract implementation
item.description: Ukraine's president set a one-week deadline for completing the legal groundwork for a Patriot air defense agreement, warning of "serious personnel decisions" if progress stalls.

item.title: 🚁 Ukraine releases footage of drones striking Russian warship at Baltic Fleet base near St. Petersburg
item.description: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces published video of drones attacking a Russian warship armed with guided missiles at the Kronstadt naval base, demonstrating Kyiv's reach into Russia's Baltic waters.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden prepares special prisons for 13-year-old gang killers amid Nordic crime surge
item.description: Swedish authorities are building specialized prison facilities for teenagers recruited by gangs to carry out murders and bombings, as organized crime networks increasingly target minors across Scandinavia.

item.title: 🇰🇼 One killed, dozens wounded in Iranian attack on Kuwait International Airport
item.description: Iranian drone and missile strikes hit Kuwait's international airport, killing one person and wounding more than 60 others, as regional conflict escalates despite ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇬🇭 Ghana passes sweeping law criminalizing LGBTQ+ activity amid public panic
item.description: Ghana has passed a sweeping law criminalizing LGBTQ+ activity, with people panicking over the new restrictions in the West African nation.
